etymology.description: The phrase combines the individual characters 我(我) (wǒ, "I"), 不(不) (bù, "no; not"), and 知道(知道) (zhīdào, "to know"). 我(我) (wǒ, "I") indicates the speaker themselves. 不(不) (bù, "no; not") negates the action or state. 知道(知道) (zhīdào, "to know") describes having knowledge or awareness.
etymology.interpretation: The phrase 我不知道 (wǒ bù zhīdào, "I don't know") straightforwardly negates the state of knowing by combining 我(我) (wǒ, "I") and 不(不) (bù, "not") with 知道(知道) (zhīdào, "to know"). 我(我) (wǒ, "I") identifies the subject; while 知道(知道) (zhīdào, "to know") conveys the idea of having knowledge. By adding 不(不) (bù, "not"), it forms the simple negation of this knowledge.

examples.chinese: 他问我问题(他問我問題)，我不知道答案(我不知道答案)。
examples.pinyin: Tā wèn wǒ wèntí, wǒ bù zhīdào dá'àn.
examples.english: He asked me a question, I don't know the answer.

examples.chinese: 如果你问我这个(如果你問我這個)，我不知道(我不知道)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ wèn wǒ zhège, wǒ bù zhīdào.
examples.english: If you ask me this, I don't know.
